title: Apply: thermal energy and equilibrium
description: Apply your understanding of thermal energy and thermal equilibrium in this set of free practice questions.

assessment_items.question_content:
An [[☃ definition 1]] consists of Object $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ and Object $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$. Each object is a subsystem made up of particles. The two subsystems have different masses, volumes, and starting temperatures, as shown in the model below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the explanation of what happens when the objects are placed in contact.**

Because $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ has a [[☃ dropdown 1]] than $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$, thermal energy flows from [[☃ dropdown 2]]. 

The average kinetic energy of the particles in $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ [[☃ dropdown 3]] and the average kinetic energy of the particles in $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$ [[☃ dropdown 4]] until the [[☃ dropdown 5]] of both objects is the same.
hints: When two objects at different temperatures are placed in contact, thermal energy spontaneously transfers from the warmer to the cooler object. The thermal energy that transfers is called **heat**.
Since $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ initially has a higher temperature, thermal energy flows from $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ to $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$.

As thermal energy flows from $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ to $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$, the average kinetic energy of the particles in $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ decreases and the average kinetic energy of the particles in $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$ increases.

The average kinetic energy of the particles in a system is measured as **temperature**. Eventually, enough thermal energy transfers from $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ to $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$ that their temperatures become equal $(T_A=T_B)$. At this temperature, there is no longer a net thermal energy flow between the objects. The particles of both objects have the same average kinetic energy.

Therefore, the correct answers are:

Because A has a higher temperature than B, thermal energy flows from A to B. 

The average kinetic energy of the particles in A decreases and the average kinetic energy of the particles in B increases until the temperature of both objects is the same.

options.definition: An isolated system cannot transfer energy or matter with its surroundings. The total amount of energy in an isolated system does not change.

options.alt:
A large red cube with the letter A above it. Below the red cube is text stating T subscript A equals twenty five degrees C and m subscript A equals fifty g.

To the right of A is a smaller blue cube with the letter B above it. Below the blue cube is text stating T subscript B equals ten degrees C and m subscript B equals thirty five g.

assessment_items.question_content:
An [[☃ definition 1]] consists of two subsystems, Subsystem $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ and Subsystem $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$. Each subsystem is made up of particles. $\color{red}{\text{A}}$ and $\color{blue}{\text{B}}$ are at different initial temperatures. They are placed in contact with each other.


**Which of the following graphs correctly models how the temperatures of the two subsystems will change over time?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: An isolated system cannot transfer energy or matter with its surroundings. The total amount of energy in an isolated system does not change.

choices.content: ![A graph with vertical y-axis labeled as temperature and horizontal x-axis labeled as time. A red line labeled A starts high on the vertical axis and gradually curves downward. A blue line labeled B starts low on the vertical axis and gradually curves upward. Both lines merge at the same time and temperature, and continue horizontally.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9cabfc1a9bdd795e82d32f6312cfa67f.svg)

![A graph with vertical y-axis labeled as temperature and horizontal x-axis labeled as time.  A red line labeled A starts high on the vertical axis and curves downward very steeply until it levels off horizontally. A blue line labeled B  starts low on the vertical axis and curves upward until it levels off horizontally. Line A levels off before Line B. Eventually the two lines merge.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/efeace4a569675eaeb291adbb82c9152.svg)

![A graph with vertical y-axis labeled as temperature and horizontal x-axis labeled as time. A red line labeled A starts high on the vertical axis and curves downward until it levels off horizontally. A blue line labeled B starts low on the vertical axis and curves upward until it levels off horizontally. The two lines cross early and then level off at different temperatures.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/218c51925101fb6250388dd876187444.svg)

assessment_items.question_content:
A child’s swimming pool holds $100$ liters of water. A nearby pond contains over $10{,}000$ times as much water. On a warm summer day, the water molecules in both systems have the same average kinetic energy. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statements.**

* The temperature of the pool water is [[☃ dropdown 1]] the temperature of the pond water.

* The total thermal energy contained in the pool is [[☃ dropdown 2]] the total thermal energy contained in the pond.
hints: Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles of a system. So if the water molecules of both systems have the same average kinetic energy, they have the same temperature.

Thermal energy is the kinetic energy of randomly moving particles. The more particles a system contains, the more thermal energy that system contains. The swimming pool contains fewer water molecules than the pond, so it contains less thermal energy.

So the answers are:

* The temperature of the pool water is equal to the temperature of the pond water.

* The total thermal energy contained in the pool is less than the total thermal energy of the pond.

options.alt: A photo of a small child in a small plastic swimming pool.
options.caption: *Hard at work studying thermodynamics*

assessment_items.question_content:
Two cast-iron skillets are heated on a stove. The larger skillet has twice the mass of the smaller skillet. After heating, the iron atoms in both skillets have the same average kinetic energy. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statements.**

* The temperature of the larger skillet is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the temperature of the smaller skillet.

* The total thermal energy contained in the larger skillet is [[☃ dropdown 4]] the total thermal energy contained in the smaller skillet.
hints: Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles of a system. So if the iron atoms of both systems have the same average kinetic energy, they have the same temperature.

Thermal energy is the kinetic energy of randomly moving particles. The more particles a system contains, the more thermal energy that system contains. The larger skillet contains more iron atoms than the smaller skillet, so it contains more thermal energy.

So the answers are:

* The temperature of the larger skillet is equal to the temperature of the smaller skillet.

* The total thermal energy contained in the larger skillet is greater than the total thermal energy contained in the smaller skillet.

options.alt: A photo of a stacked iron skillets of various sizes.
options.caption: *Cast-iron skillets of varying sizes*

assessment_items.question_content:
A set of marbles are made of the same type of glass. A child leaves two marbles outside on a cold day: a small marble, and a large marble with five times the mass of the small marble. An hour later, the particles in both marbles possess the same average kinetic energy. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**Complete the statements.**

* The temperature of the smaller marble is [[☃ dropdown 3]] the temperature of the larger marble.

* The total thermal energy contained in the smaller marble is [[☃ dropdown 4]] the total thermal energy contained in the larger marble.
hints: Temperature is a measure of the average kinetic energy of the particles of a system. So if the particles of both systems have the same average kinetic energy, they have the same temperature.

Thermal energy is the kinetic energy of randomly moving particles. The more particles a system contains, the more thermal energy that system contains. The smaller marble contains fewer particles than the larger marble, so it contains less thermal energy.

So the answers are:

* The temperature of the smaller marble is equal to the temperature of the larger marble.

* The total thermal energy contained in the smaller marble is less than the total thermal energy contained in the larger marble.

options.alt: A close-up shot of five colorful glass marbles of varying sizes and designs, resting on a white, textured surface.
options.caption: *A set of lost marbles*

assessment_items.question_content:
A metal spoon is initially at room temperature. It’s placed into a cup of hot coffee that’s significantly above room temperature. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The coffee and spoon are NOT an isolated system—energy can flow between them and the surroundings.

**Complete the statements.**

* Before the spoon is placed in the hot coffee, there is [[☃ dropdown 1]].

* Just after the spoon is placed into the hot coffee, there is [[☃ dropdown 2]].

* After the spoon reaches thermal equilibrium with the coffee, thermal energy [[☃ dropdown 3]] until the spoon and coffee reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.
hints: When two objects at different temperatures are placed in contact, thermal energy spontaneously transfers from the warmer to the cooler object. The thermal energy that transfers is called **heat**.
Because the spoon is initially at room temperature, the spoon is in thermal equilibrium with its surroundings. There is no net transfer of thermal energy between the spoon and its surroundings.

When the spoon is placed into the hot coffee, there’s a temperature difference between the spoon and coffee. Thermal energy transfers from the warmer coffee to the cooler spoon until they reach thermal equilibrium.

After the spoon has reached thermal equilibrium with the coffee, they are both still warmer than the room. So thermal energy transfers from the spoon and coffee to the surroundings until a new thermal equilibrium is reached. The spoon and the coffee eventually reach room temperature together.

So, the correct answers are: 

* Before the spoon is placed in the hot coffee, there is no net transfer of thermal energy between the spoon and surroundings.

* Just after the spoon is placed into the hot coffee, there is a transfer of thermal energy from the coffee to the spoon. 

* After the spoon reaches thermal equilibrium with the coffee, thermal energy transfers from the spoon and coffee to the surroundings until the spoon and coffee reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.

choices.content: a transfer of thermal energy from the spoon to the surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the surroundings to the spoon
no net transfer of thermal energy between the spoon and surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the spoon to the coffee
a transfer of thermal energy from the coffee to the spoon
no net transfer of thermal energy between the spoon and the coffee
transfers from the spoon and coffee to the surroundings
transfers from the surroundings to the coffee and spoon

options.alt: A cup of coffee with a metal spoon in the coffee.
options.caption: *Hot coffee with spoon*

assessment_items.question_content:
Icy cold lemonade is poured into an empty, room-temperature glass. The drink sits on the counter for several hours.

[[☃ image 1]]

* Before the cold lemonade is poured into the glass, there is [[☃ dropdown 1]].

* Just after the cold lemonade is poured into the glass, there is [[☃ dropdown 2]].

* After the glass reaches thermal equilibrium with the lemonade, thermal energy [[☃ dropdown 3]] until the glass and the lemonade reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.
hints: When two objects at different temperatures are placed in contact, thermal energy spontaneously transfers from the warmer to the cooler object. The thermal energy that transfers is called **heat**.
Because the glass is initially at room temperature, the glass is in thermal equilibrium with its surroundings. There is no net transfer of thermal energy between the glass and its surroundings.

When the cold lemonade is poured into the glass, there’s a temperature difference between the lemonade and glass. Thermal energy transfers from the warmer glass to the cooler lemonade until they reach thermal equilibrium.

After the glass has reached thermal equilibrium with the lemonade, they are both still cooler than the room. So thermal energy transfers from the surroundings to the glass and the lemonade until a new thermal equilibrium is reached. The glass and the lemonade eventually reach room temperature together.

So, the correct answers are: 

* Before the cold lemonade is poured into the glass, there is no net transfer of thermal energy between the glass and surroundings.

* Just after the cold lemonade is poured into the glass, there is a transfer of thermal energy from the glass to the lemonade.

* After the glass reaches thermal equilibrium with the lemonade, thermal energy transfers from the surroundings to the glass and the lemonade until the glass and the lemonade reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.

choices.content: a transfer of thermal energy from the glass to the surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the surroundings to the glass
no net transfer of thermal energy between the glass and surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the lemonade to the glass
a transfer of thermal energy from the glass to the lemonade
no net transfer of thermal energy between the lemonade and the glass
transfers from the glass and the lemonade to the surroundings
transfers from the surroundings to the glass and the lemonade

assessment_items.question_content:
A wooden bowl is at room temperature. A student pours some hot soup into the bowl, then walks away and forgets about the soup. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The bowl and soup are NOT an isolated system—energy can flow between them and the surroundings.

**Complete the statements.**

* Before the hot soup is poured into the bowl, there is [[☃ dropdown 1]].

* Just after the hot soup is poured into the bowl, there is [[☃ dropdown 2]].

* After the bowl reaches thermal equilibrium with the soup, thermal energy [[☃ dropdown 3]] until the bowl and the soup reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.
hints: When two objects at different temperatures are placed in contact, thermal energy spontaneously transfers from the warmer to the cooler object. The thermal energy that transfers is called **heat**.
Because the bowl is initially at room temperature, the bowl is in thermal equilibrium with its surroundings. There is no net transfer of thermal energy between the bowl and its surroundings.

When the hot soup is poured into the bowl, there’s a temperature difference between the bowl and soup. Thermal energy is transferred from the warmer soup to the cooler bowl until they reach thermal equilibrium.

After the bowl has reached thermal equilibrium with the soup, they are both still warmer than the room. So thermal energy transfers from the bowl and soup to the surroundings until a new thermal equilibrium is reached. The bowl and the soup eventually reach room temperature together.

So, the correct answers are: 

* Before the hot soup is poured into the bowl, there is no net transfer of thermal energy between the bowl and surroundings.

* Just after the hot soup is poured into the bowl, there is  a transfer of thermal energy from the soup to the bowl.

* After the bowl reaches thermal equilibrium with the soup, thermal energy transfers from the bowl and the soup to the surroundings until the bowl and the soup reach thermal equilibrium with the surroundings.

choices.content: a transfer of thermal energy from the bowl to the surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the surroundings to the bowl
no net transfer of thermal energy between the bowl and surroundings
a transfer of thermal energy from the soup to the bowl
a transfer of thermal energy from the bowl to the soup
no net transfer of thermal energy between the bowl and the soup
transfers from the bowl and the soup to the surroundings
transfers from the surroundings to the bowl and the soup

options.alt: A wooden bowl filled with soup.
options.caption: *A forgotten bowl of soup*
